description:
Replace uniform-ABI any-key dispatch for indirect targets (closures, spawn, receive, continuations) with per-typed-shape dispatch.

## Why

Closures, Spawn targets, Receive handlers, and Continuations all dispatch through their callee's any-key SpecId today — the trampoline / closure stub / scheduler only knows the FnId, not which specific spec to invoke. This forces every fn reachable via these paths to keep an any-key, AND any fn called from inside an any-key body needs ITS callees' any-keys too (since the any-key body types everything as `any`).

Concretely (from .29.10 investigation):
- map_l is direct-called only; in principle no any-key needed.
- But map_l's body has a closure call whose continuation k_3 is referenced via Cont — k_3 must keep its any-key for trampoline dispatch.
- k_3's any-key body, compiled under all-any, calls map_l with [any, any].
- Without map_l's any-key, that call has no covering spec.
- The transitive closure of "needs any-key" walks every reachable fn, defeating .29.10's optimization.

## The fix

Make indirect dispatch type-aware:

- **Closures**: closure heap object stores stub_fp keyed by (FnId, capture_types, arg_types, ret_type). MakeClosure picks the typed stub matching the lambda's arrow Descr. CallClosure dispatches through it with the typed signature.
- **Spawn**: spawn-thunk dispatches through the typed closure ABI.
- **Receive**: receiver fn dispatched through a typed cont stub.
- **Continuations**: cont frame stores a SpecId (not just an FnId). Trampoline reads the SpecId, dispatches to that exact compiled spec.

Once dispatch is typed, no indirect-target needs an any-key (typed stubs cover all the live cases). The optimization in .29.10 becomes truly trivial: any-keys only needed for entry-point-like fns with no caller.

## Scope

- Closure heap layout: add typed stub_fp(s).
- Cont frame layout: add SpecId field.
- Trampoline + scheduler hooks: read SpecId from cont frame; dispatch via it.
- MakeClosure / emit_call / emit_call_closure / receive / spawn lowering: pick the typed stub matching the dispatch shape.
- ir_codegen closure_shapes: expand key from (FnId, capture_count) to (FnId, capture_types, arity, ret_kind).

Substantial refactor — likely its own epic with multiple subtickets. File children as the work decomposes.

## Out of scope

- AOT vs JIT differences in closure ABI: handled inside the same lowering path.
- Cross-module dispatch (deferred to a separate cross-module concern).

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-ul4.29.10 (any-key on-demand): becomes trivial once indirect dispatch is typed.
title: Typed dispatch for closures, spawn, receive, continuations
